rules._comment_root: Default-deny is too disruptive for this app — every existing screen reads broad subtrees (chats, users, tickets, ferra*). We keep the existing auth-only baseline but tighten /dms with per-pair participant checks. Note: a signed-in user reading /commonComm/dms wholesale still succeeds under the auth-only root rule. The intended pattern (and what the dashboard does) is to fan out to /dms/{pairKey} per-pair where the participant check IS enforced. Tightening the wholesale read requires a per-user dm index (e.g. /userDms/{uid}/{pairKey}: true) — tracked as a v2 follow-up.

$pairKey._comment: Reads and writes scoped to the two uids listed in meta/participants. A user creating a fresh DM must include themselves in newData.meta.participants — see the .validate on participants below.
$pairKey..write: auth != null && (data.child('meta/participants/' + auth.uid).val() === true || newData.child('meta/participants/' + auth.uid).val() === true)

config._comment: Allowlist + team-member directory. Locked to admins on the client side; the rule here just ensures auth.
